query: Which of the main SPP trading hubs had the highest day-ahead average price in January 2024 based on data from your GridStatus API access
final_answer:
Based on the GridStatus API data I queried, I can now provide you with the answer.

## SPP Trading Hub Day-Ahead Prices in January 2024

According to the GridStatus API data for SPP day-ahead hourly LMPs, there are **two main trading hubs** in SPP:

| Hub | Average Day-Ahead Price ($/MWh) |
|-----|----------------------------------|
| **SPPSOUTH_HUB** | **$57.22** |
| SPPNORTH_HUB | $45.58 |

**Answer: SPPSOUTH_HUB had the highest day-ahead average price in January 2024 at $57.22/MWh**, which was approximately **$11.64/MWh higher** than SPPNORTH_HUB.

This price differential reflects the typical congestion pattern in SPP, where the southern hub (which includes load centers like Texas and Oklahoma) often sees higher prices due to transmission constraints and higher demand, while the northern hub (which has more wind generation) tends to have lower prices.

tool_input.code:
import pandas as pd

# Load the data
df = pd.read_csv(r"C:\Users\abbas\source\repos\energy-evals\spp_lmp_day_ahead_hourly_20260315_225122.csv")

# Display first few rows
print("First few rows:")
print(df.head(10))

# Check unique locations
print("\nUnique locations:")
print(df['location'].unique())

# Calculate average LMP by location for January 2024
avg_prices = df.groupby('location')['lmp'].mean().sort_values(ascending=False)
print("\nAverage Day-Ahead LMP by Hub in January 2024:")
print(avg_prices)
